'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:請田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11631810}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = Uketa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|270px]]
Honden (left) and haiden (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Hozu-chō Tateiwa 4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|56.63|N|135|36|27.66|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uketa Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Matsuo Shrine / Uketa Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uketa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Hozukyō (Kameoka).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}}}{{small|Uketa Shrine stands on the left bank; beyond the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} on the right bank is {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}.}}]] '''Uketa Shrine''' (うけたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hozu-chō Tateiwa, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. It stands in eastern {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, at the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}, where the river's name changes from "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōi River|1=UNKNOWN}}" to "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hozu River|1=UNKNOWN}}." Across the river stands {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}. Together with Kuwata Shrine, it is connected with traditions about the excavation of Hozu Gorge. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (おおやまくいのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) - also called Sayorihime-no-Mikoto. === History === Because shrine buildings and documents were destroyed by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}-{{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), detailed origins are unclearExplanatory board in the precincts.. The enshrined deity Ōyamakui-no-Kami is said to be a deity who came from Izumo to develop the Tanba region, then a lake area, and to have begun excavating Hozu Gorge from the site of {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|1=Kuwada Shrine}} opposite Uketa Shrine. It is said the shrine name "Uketa" comes from accepting (uketa) this groundbreaking hoe-rite for that development. Traditions that the Kameoka Basin was once a lake remain in many nearby places (see "{{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin#Lake tradition|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}" for details). Shrine tradition gives its foundation as {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}). Because the shrine site was called "Iwao," it is identified as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Ishio Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

[[File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|270px]]
Honden (left) and haiden (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Hozu-chō Tateiwa 4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|56.63|N|135|36|27.66|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uketa Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Matsuo Shrine / Uketa Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uketa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Hozukyō (Kameoka).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}}}{{small|Uketa Shrine stands on the left bank; beyond the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} on the right bank is {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}.}}]] '''Uketa Shrine''' (うけたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hozu-chō Tateiwa, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. It stands in eastern {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, at the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}, where the river's name changes from "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōi River|1=UNKNOWN}}" to "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hozu River|1=UNKNOWN}}." Across the river stands {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}. Together with Kuwata Shrine, it is connected with traditions about the excavation of Hozu Gorge. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (おおやまくいのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) - also called Sayorihime-no-Mikoto. === History === Because shrine buildings and documents were destroyed by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}-{{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), detailed origins are unclearExplanatory board in the precincts.. The enshrined deity Ōyamakui-no-Kami is said to be a deity who came from Izumo to develop the Tanba region, then a lake area, and to have begun excavating Hozu Gorge from the site of {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|1=Kuwada Shrine}} opposite Uketa Shrine. It is said the shrine name "Uketa" comes from accepting (uketa) this groundbreaking hoe-rite for that development. Traditions that the Kameoka Basin was once a lake remain in many nearby places (see "{{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin#Lake tradition|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}" for details). Shrine tradition gives its foundation as {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}). Because the shrine site was called "Iwao," it is identified as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Ishio Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Matsuo Shrine / Uketa Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uketa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Hozukyō (Kameoka).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}}}{{small|Uketa Shrine stands on the left bank; beyond the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} on the right bank is {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}.}}]] '''Uketa Shrine''' (うけたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hozu-chō Tateiwa, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. It stands in eastern {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, at the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}, where the river's name changes from "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōi River|1=UNKNOWN}}" to "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hozu River|1=UNKNOWN}}." Across the river stands {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}. Together with Kuwata Shrine, it is connected with traditions about the excavation of Hozu Gorge. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (おおやまくいのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) - also called Sayorihime-no-Mikoto. === History === Because shrine buildings and documents were destroyed by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}-{{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), detailed origins are unclearExplanatory board in the precincts.. The enshrined deity Ōyamakui-no-Kami is said to be a deity who came from Izumo to develop the Tanba region, then a lake area, and to have begun excavating Hozu Gorge from the site of {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|1=Kuwada Shrine}} opposite Uketa Shrine. It is said the shrine name "Uketa" comes from accepting (uketa) this groundbreaking hoe-rite for that development. Traditions that the Kameoka Basin was once a lake remain in many nearby places (see "{{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin#Lake tradition|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}" for details). Shrine tradition gives its foundation as {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}). Because the shrine site was called "Iwao," it is identified as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Ishio Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:うけたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

[[File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|270px]]
Honden (left) and haiden (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Hozu-chō Tateiwa 4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|56.63|N|135|36|27.66|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uketa Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Matsuo Shrine / Uketa Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uketa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Hozukyō (Kameoka).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}}}{{small|Uketa Shrine stands on the left bank; beyond the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} on the right bank is {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}.}}]] '''Uketa Shrine''' (うけたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hozu-chō Tateiwa, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. It stands in eastern {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, at the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}, where the river's name changes from "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōi River|1=UNKNOWN}}" to "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hozu River|1=UNKNOWN}}." Across the river stands {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}. Together with Kuwata Shrine, it is connected with traditions about the excavation of Hozu Gorge. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (おおやまくいのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) - also called Sayorihime-no-Mikoto. === History === Because shrine buildings and documents were destroyed by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}-{{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), detailed origins are unclearExplanatory board in the precincts.. The enshrined deity Ōyamakui-no-Kami is said to be a deity who came from Izumo to develop the Tanba region, then a lake area, and to have begun excavating Hozu Gorge from the site of {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|1=Kuwada Shrine}} opposite Uketa Shrine. It is said the shrine name "Uketa" comes from accepting (uketa) this groundbreaking hoe-rite for that development. Traditions that the Kameoka Basin was once a lake remain in many nearby places (see "{{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin#Lake tradition|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}" for details). Shrine tradition gives its foundation as {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}). Because the shrine site was called "Iwao," it is identified as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Ishio Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:亀岡市の建築物]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:請田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11631810}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = Uketa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|270px]]
Honden (left) and haiden (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Hozu-chō Tateiwa 4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|56.63|N|135|36|27.66|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uketa Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Matsuo Shrine / Uketa Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uketa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Hozukyō (Kameoka).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}}}{{small|Uketa Shrine stands on the left bank; beyond the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} on the right bank is {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}.}}]] '''Uketa Shrine''' (うけたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hozu-chō Tateiwa, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. It stands in eastern {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, at the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}, where the river's name changes from "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōi River|1=UNKNOWN}}" to "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hozu River|1=UNKNOWN}}." Across the river stands {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}. Together with Kuwata Shrine, it is connected with traditions about the excavation of Hozu Gorge. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (おおやまくいのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) - also called Sayorihime-no-Mikoto. === History === Because shrine buildings and documents were destroyed by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}-{{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), detailed origins are unclearExplanatory board in the precincts.. The enshrined deity Ōyamakui-no-Kami is said to be a deity who came from Izumo to develop the Tanba region, then a lake area, and to have begun excavating Hozu Gorge from the site of {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|1=Kuwada Shrine}} opposite Uketa Shrine. It is said the shrine name "Uketa" comes from accepting (uketa) this groundbreaking hoe-rite for that development. Traditions that the Kameoka Basin was once a lake remain in many nearby places (see "{{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin#Lake tradition|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}" for details). Shrine tradition gives its foundation as {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}). Because the shrine site was called "Iwao," it is identified as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Ishio Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:京都府の神社]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} [[JA:請田神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11631810}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2026-01}} {{神社 |名称 = Uketa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|270px]]
Honden (left) and haiden (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Hozu-chō Tateiwa 4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|56.63|N|135|36|27.66|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uketa Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Matsuo Shrine / Uketa Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uketa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Hozukyō (Kameoka).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}}}{{small|Uketa Shrine stands on the left bank; beyond the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} on the right bank is {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}.}}]] '''Uketa Shrine''' (うけたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hozu-chō Tateiwa, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. It stands in eastern {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, at the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}, where the river's name changes from "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōi River|1=UNKNOWN}}" to "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hozu River|1=UNKNOWN}}." Across the river stands {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}. Together with Kuwata Shrine, it is connected with traditions about the excavation of Hozu Gorge. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (おおやまくいのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) - also called Sayorihime-no-Mikoto. === History === Because shrine buildings and documents were destroyed by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}-{{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), detailed origins are unclearExplanatory board in the precincts.. The enshrined deity Ōyamakui-no-Kami is said to be a deity who came from Izumo to develop the Tanba region, then a lake area, and to have begun excavating Hozu Gorge from the site of {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|1=Kuwada Shrine}} opposite Uketa Shrine. It is said the shrine name "Uketa" comes from accepting (uketa) this groundbreaking hoe-rite for that development. Traditions that the Kameoka Basin was once a lake remain in many nearby places (see "{{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin#Lake tradition|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}" for details). Shrine tradition gives its foundation as {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}). Because the shrine site was called "Iwao," it is identified as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Ishio Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:丹波国の式内社]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Traditions that the Kameoka Basin was once a lake remain in many nearby places (see "{{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin#Lake tradition|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}" for details). Shrine tradition gives its foundation as {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}). Because the shrine site was called "Iwao," it is identified as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Ishio Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Honden (left) and haiden (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Hozu-chō Tateiwa 4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|56.63|N|135|36|27.66|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uketa Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}

It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. It stands in eastern {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, at the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}, where the river's name changes from "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōi River|1=UNKNOWN}}" to "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hozu River|1=UNKNOWN}}." Across the river stands {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}. Together with Kuwata Shrine, it is connected with traditions about the excavation of Hozu Gorge. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (おおやまくいのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) - also called Sayorihime-no-Mikoto. === History === Because shrine buildings and documents were destroyed by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}-{{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), detailed origins are unclearExplanatory board in the precincts.. The enshrined deity Ōyamakui-no-Kami is said to be a deity who came from Izumo to develop the Tanba region, then a lake area, and to have begun excavating Hozu Gorge from the site of {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|1=Kuwada Shrine}} opposite Uketa Shrine. It is said the shrine name "Uketa" comes from accepting (uketa) this groundbreaking hoe-rite for that development. Traditions that the Kameoka Basin was once a lake remain in many nearby places (see "{{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin#Lake tradition|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}" for details). Shrine tradition gives its foundation as {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}). Because the shrine site was called "Iwao," it is identified as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Ishio Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]

[[File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) keidai.JPG|270px]]
Honden (left) and haiden (right) |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}Hozu-chō Tateiwa 4 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|00|56.63|N|135|36|27.66|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Uketa Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Mikoto|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}
{{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small) {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} |創建 = (trad.) {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Matsuo Shrine / Uketa Daimyōjin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 20|Q2959|WD=Q2959|1=October 20}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto|label=Uketa Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Hozukyō (Kameoka).JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}}}{{small|Uketa Shrine stands on the left bank; beyond the {{ill|lt=Sagano Line|Q1073011|WD=Q1073011|1=Sagano Line}} on the right bank is {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}.}}]] '''Uketa Shrine''' (うけたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hozu-chō Tateiwa, {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, {{ill|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|Q120730|WD=Q120730|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}}. It stands in eastern {{ill|lt=Kameoka|Q633996|WD=Q633996|1=Kameoka, Kyoto}}, at the entrance to {{ill|lt=Hozu Gorge|Q10887680|WD=Q10887680|1=Hozu Gorge}}, where the river's name changes from "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ōi River|1=UNKNOWN}}" to "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hozu River|1=UNKNOWN}}." Across the river stands {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine#Kuwata Shrine (Kameoka Shinomachi Yamamoto)|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|lt=Kuwata Shrine|1=Kuwada Shrine}}. Together with Kuwata Shrine, it is connected with traditions about the excavation of Hozu Gorge. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|WD=Q10935105|lt=Ōyamakui-no-Kami|Q10935105|WD=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}} (おおやまくいのかみ) * {{ill|lt=Ichikishimahime|Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|WD=Q11059454|lt=Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto|1=Ichikishima-hime}} (いちきしまひめのみこと) - also called Sayorihime-no-Mikoto. === History === Because shrine buildings and documents were destroyed by military fire in the {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|WD=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} era ({{ill|lt=1558|Q6601|WD=Q6601|1=1558}}-{{ill|lt=1570|Q6659|WD=Q6659|1=1570}}), detailed origins are unclearExplanatory board in the precincts.. The enshrined deity Ōyamakui-no-Kami is said to be a deity who came from Izumo to develop the Tanba region, then a lake area, and to have begun excavating Hozu Gorge from the site of {{ill|lt=Kuwata Shrine|Q11537594|WD=Q11537594|1=Kuwada Shrine}} opposite Uketa Shrine. It is said the shrine name "Uketa" comes from accepting (uketa) this groundbreaking hoe-rite for that development. Traditions that the Kameoka Basin was once a lake remain in many nearby places (see "{{ill|lt=Kameoka Basin#Lake tradition|Q11181997|WD=Q11181997|1=Kameoka Basin}}" for details). Shrine tradition gives its foundation as {{ill|lt=Wadō|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 2 ({{ill|lt=709|Q23514|WD=Q23514|1=709}}). Because the shrine site was called "Iwao," it is identified as a {{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} for the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the mid-{{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|WD=Q193292|1=Heian period}} {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}} {{ill|lt=Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as "Ishio Shrine" in {{ill|lt=Kuwata District|Q11537600|WD=Q11537600|1=Kuwada district}}, {{ill|lt=Tanba Province|Q954511|WD=Q954511|1=Tanba Province}}. For unclear reasons, there is also a theory identifying it as Matsuo Shrine, another shikinaisha of Kuwata DistrictOther candidate shrines for Matsuo Shrine include {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)|Q11529679|WD=Q11529679|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsuo Shrine (Kameoka)}} in Asahi-chō, Kameoka.. After the destruction by fire in the Eiroku era, it was moved to Miyanoue (present Hozu Hachimangū)''Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'', entry for Hozu village.. It returned to the current site in the {{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} era ({{ill|lt=1624|Q6821|WD=Q6821|1=1624}}-{{ill|lt=1645|Q6925|WD=Q6925|1=1645}}), but at Miyanoue a divided spirit is still enshrined as a {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}}. In the early modern period, Hozu Gorge was called "Uketa Gorge," and with Uketa Shrine, the Kuwata Shrine on the opposite bank was also called "Uketa Shrine" or "Uketa Shrine (floating-field shrine)." === Precincts === File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) honden.JPG|Honden File:Uketa-jinja (Kameoka) torii.JPG|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === '''Precinct sub-shrine''' * Inari Shrine - located beside the honden. '''Off-site shrine''' [[File:Hozu-hachimangū keidai.JPG|thumb|200px|right|{{center|Hozu Hachimangū precincts}}{{small|Hachimangū at back left; Uketa Shrine {{ill|lt=tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=tongū}} at right. Both are Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties.}}]] * {{ill|lt=Tongū|Q11664877|WD=Q11664877|1=Tongū}} *: Located in the precincts of Hozu Hachimangū (21 Miyanoue, Hozu-chō, Kameoka). Uketa Shrine is said to have been temporarily moved here from the Eiroku era to the Kan'ei era, and after returning, a divided spirit continued to be enshrined here as a tongū ({{ill|lt=otabisho|Q11489117|WD=Q11489117|1=Otabisho}}). Its style is one-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}}. Hachimangū is said to have drifted here from Hosokawa village in Kita-Kuwata District during the Kan'ei era and was rebuilt on the former Uketa Shrine site after Uketa's return. On the hill to the north remains a bell tower as a relic of Wakōin, the former shrine-temple (now abolished){{ill|lt=jingūji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}}. These three structures, Hozu Hachimangū's honden and bell tower and Uketa Shrine's tongū, are designated cultural properties of Kyoto Prefecture[http://www.kyoto-be.ne.jp/bunkazai/cms/?page_id=200 Kyoto Prefecture designated/registered cultural properties] (Kyoto Prefectural Board of Education).[https://www.city.kameoka.kyoto.jp/shakai/bunkazai/hozuhachiman-uketa.html New Kyoto Prefecture cultural-property designation for Kameoka cultural assets] (Kameoka City website).. === Festivals === * Autumn Grand Festival ({{ill|lt=October 21|Q2960|WD=Q2960|1=October 21}}) *: Also called the "Hozu Fire Festival." It begins with the eve festival; the divine spirit is welcomed from the main sanctuary to the tongū within Hozu Hachimangū. === Cultural properties === === Kyoto Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Uketa Shrine Tongū - designated March 24, 2015 (Heisei 27). === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === References === {{small| (Sources used in writing this article) }} * Explanatory board in the precincts * ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Place Names of Kyoto Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}), Kameoka City, Hozu village entry * Shikinaisha Research Association (ed.), ''Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 18'' (Kōgakkan University Press), Ishio Shrine entry === Related literature === {{small| (Related literature not used in writing this article) }} {{Commonscat|Uketa-jinja (Kameoka)}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1923|chapter=[{{NDLDC|976611/53}} Hozu Village Uketa Shrine and Hachimangū]|title=Kyoto Prefectural Survey Committee Report on Historic Sites and Scenic Places, Volume 4|publisher=Kyoto Prefecture|isbn=|ref=}} - Link is to the National Diet Library Digital Collections. {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Uketa Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[category:亀岡市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tanba Province]] [[Category:丹波湖伝説]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kameoka, Kyoto]]
